title: Reattaching a loose board
materials: cartridge-paper-130 for inner lining strip if needed
sources: Conservator practice documented in Carolyn Horton, Cleaning and Preserving Bindings (1969 ALA guide, copyright, structural description only). Draws on repair principles in Cockerell, Bookbinding and the Care of Books (1901).
notes: Reattaching a detached board where the hinge is broken but the cover cloth/paper is still present. Two methods: (1) Japanese tissue hinge — for boards that are barely detached or where the original cloth hinge is still partially sound. Apply a strip of kozo tissue with mixture paste along the inside joint, work it in while damp, press under weight. (2) Linen-thread tipping — for boards that are fully detached. Sew a strip of linen around the bookblock spine, glue the board to it. Both methods described. When NOT to attempt repair: red rot, full-spine separation, vellum covers.
